title: Kafka Consumer Lag Spike Causing Event Processing Delays
symptoms: Consumer lag on order-events topic exceeding 2.4 million messages
Consumer group showing repeated rebalance events every 30 seconds
Individual consumer instances showing long GC pauses over 45 seconds
Order processing throughput dropped from 15K/min to near zero
Burr consumer lag monitoring alerts firing continuously
root_cause: Consumer group rebalance storm triggered by GC pauses exceeding the session timeout (30s) on one consumer instance, causing repeated group coordinator disruptions
resolution: Increased consumer session timeout to 120 seconds, tuned JVM garbage collection parameters, added a dedicated consumer instance, and manually reset consumer offsets to drain the lag
preventive_actions: Tune JVM GC parameters for all Kafka consumer services
Increase session timeout to accommodate worst-case GC pauses
Implement consumer lag alerting at 100K and 500K message thresholds
Add consumer group stability monitoring for rebalance frequency
Deploy Kafka consumer auto-scaling based on lag metrics

timeline.event: Consumer lag begins climbing on order-events topic
Consumer group rebalance detected by monitoring
Lag alert fires at 100K message threshold
Priya Sharma begins investigation, identifies rebalance storm
GC pauses exceeding session timeout confirmed as trigger
Session timeout increased, GC parameters tuned
Consumer group stabilizes, lag begins draining
Lag fully drained, order processing current

embedding_summary: Kafka consumer lag spiked to 2.4 million messages on order-events topic due to consumer group rebalance storm triggered by GC pauses exceeding session timeout. Order processing delayed up to 45 minutes. Resolved by increasing session timeout, tuning GC, and adding consumer capacity.
